title: phlomis orientalis subsp. caucasica
intro: Phlomis orientalis subsp. caucasica, also known as Caucasian Jerusalem sage, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae, native to the Caucasus region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which help to soothe and calm the skin. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and acting as an antioxidant to protect against cell damage. Phlomis orientalis subsp. caucasica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and relaxation.
